reviews.review: The game is extremely simple and low-end devices could easily handle it, the graphics are amazing and the physics are satisfying. I love this game mainly because it's easy on keyboard and it takes practice and time to make progress. Personally this is everything I could ever want, better than any other game.

Game has a lot of potential. its hella fun but i have a few issues with it.
[olist]
 [*] needs some form of guide or tutorial for newbies instead of just throwing you in, or at the least something to explain what each event is and how it functions
 [*] desync def is an issue but i know the game is in early access still so devs are working on that. still a bother at times 
 [*] more things to do and interact with other plays/the open world, the grind is fun but i can see it getting boring quick but again the game is still in early access so lots of time for these things to come in
 [*] we need bodykits/more customization for the cars, there's already a huge list of cool cars all we need is more stuff to make em look cooler
[/olist]

wow another game using the same cars.  physics an maps as 2 other games.  I'm starting to think this studio is just the main studio Drift Physics Crew just renamed so they can keep trying to milk this as much as possible by reselling the game over an over along with the car DLC's after the first 2 games died within 2 months.  Just a carbon copy of Furidashi.  An the price.  Really?  30 bucks  The first games this cloned from were like 18$ but 30 for this.  I just feel ripped off.

If you don't own Furidashi or RDS go ahead if you want to spend 30$ on this clone
But if you do own them don't buy this.

Surprisingly addictive once you're familiar with everything, and very easy to drift once your car is nicely upgraded. Not to mention there's at least 40 something cars to play with, indicating masses of grinding ahead! 
I rate this above the other drift game that I enjoyed quite recently (Hanjozoku or Hanzojoku?), as there's far more variety in what you can do here, and they appear to be adding new cars/areas etc on a monthly(ish) basis.

Awesome game even before it is realeased.

Technicaly its NOT realy there yet to recommend it!
-Menues not controllable with the left stick
- freesync not working at all (even forcing through drivers does not work on amd)
- no Anti-aliasing

Gameplay-wise it lacks activities! We got a decent open world here but activities are very few here and there - some dont make any sense like driving 100 meters to deliver a pizza ?!. Feels like devs did not care and just wanted to fill it with something.

There is no real main menu - you get thrown into the server selection and can access a menu while ingame.
I guess thats a preference for everyone.

I might check it out again in 6-12 months depending on development-progress.
the game is amazing and so much fun
Loving the game so far, I play a lot of racing games (casually) and this has to have one of the most rewarding drift physics. Keep up the good work devs!

Takes some getting used to the physics, and the whole 'hit the barrier or literally anything at all and you lose all your points' can be infuriating when it bugs out or an AI car just spawns from nowhere... But, it's really fun. I've had a blast with it so far and definitely will be back more and more. It's somewhere between Forza Horizon and CarX in terms of realism in the handling, and I like that spot... Just wish there was a bit more dev time in the UI, and again, that damn combo ender!

Was well worth the price!

Tries to compete with carx, but is lacking customization as well as tuning is very limited. Currently, I am having more fun playing carx over this, especially with unlocking new cars as well as its a lot easier to earn money in carx because you have actual drift events to do, etc. This game will probably get blown out of the water when Carx Streets gets released in 2057

I used to drift alot on Forza Horizon 1/3/4 and on FiveM Velocity Drift servers and this game is very easy to pick up and get sliding

It is more on the arcade side, at the time on writing you have to manually set controller keybinds and such but Controllers, Keyboards/Mouse, and Wheels all work well with the game. I personally use a controller. 
Theres no visual parts customization other then Paint/Vinals, Rims/Tires, Neon and Mufflers. There are upgrade parts and all cars come with bodykits and the such, and IMO most are done very well. 
The Livery editor is amazing, better the Forza's, better then Heat's [imo] by far. Your not applying layers to just the side or the hood, you can seemlessly [mostly] flow layers from say the hood to the windows to the doors with little editing compaired to the other games [depending on how well you pick up the controls and such]. Theres a good amount of decals as well and some of the liverys I've seen already are amazing.

All round, I recommend the game. The price I say is a bit steap, I'd say $20 or $25 would be better but its not bad for the price. Community is great too, Shout out "Drift and Friends" crew and "Lost Souls" crew.

it's like if forza horizon was ONLY for drifting and even tho i'm terrible at drifting but i love driving around in this game. also love that there's no drivers in any of the cars! i wish there were more maps/bigger map and that the roads have to be discovered but its hitting that openworld driving itch for sure

im not really a car person but this game really does impress me with the mechanics of how this game works i do wish  we can like start events like 1v1 whoever has the most points or does the best drift without understterr or oversteer
Great physics and graphics... wish there was a campaign
Please Please Please give us an option to turn off traffic. Every time I get a decently high multiplier some stupid ai pops up. Other than that a very solid game if you don't mind grinding a lil bit.

A lovely game, just a lot of annoying features, like the AI, and the fact you lightly tap a wall and you lose all your points, as well as the fact you need to be near players for AI to be unhittable, but you can still hit players, like make up your mind, either no AI's or No players, or make an AI free zone where players are ghosted, or at least make it an option, like a cinematic mode where nothing can bother you AI and Players alike, I would like to see it that the car would be able to take a little bit more punishment before you lose your points, I lost 70.k worth of points because i did a wall tap which didn't set me off course or ruin my drift, What destroyed my drift was seeing all my hard-earned points just dissipate into nothingness and I only get like 500 out of 70.k, one time I had over 180.k points and i lost it all because a player hit me while drifting which was apparently due to de-sync, if that player was ghosted, i would be 180.k plus richer in the game. As I said, amazing game, one of the best I've seen, just very very annoying. EDIT: FOR THE LOVE OF GOD PLEASE REMOVE THE AI, YOU ARE RUINING YOUR GAME JUST BECAUSE OF THE STUPID AI.
great game would pick this over forza

Allright so... drifting is fun, upgrading and collecting cars is as well. The cars themselves are really cool looking with a few nice applicable upgrades (bot performance and optical). But there are some issues that need to be fixed. But since it's early access, I can forgive them for now. The issues (+solutions) are:

- a disconnect results in loosing your earned points (not cool)
- not that much to do besides farming currencies and delivering stuff, more single-player challenges would be nice
- cars barely steer outside of drifting making it hard to correct minor mistakes or steering in general outside of drifting
- more explanations needed: you're set free in the world without any idea what to do, how to do things etc. Just a tiny bit more hand-holding would be amazing
- no explanation why I can only have two cars in my garage (how to increase that?)
- traveling the world can be annoying, because you have to break to see a bigger map and then all roads are maze-like (navigation-functionality needed)
- oncoming traffic can be (for some) be quite annoying -> make it a option to disable collision

But that's it from me. Thus far, I am enjoying my time and can recommend this for some people wanting to experiment a bit and who love to drift.

Other than

My first drift sim with a wheel and seat combo. Super fun, car progression keeps the gameplay alive, having a server full with like minded people is what will keep this game going. I still feel that we need more drift spots but what is there is perfect so far. Car customisation seems at the right spot for a build in progress, happy to see where this goes.
Hope to see this game progress into something amazing.

Long story short, 
[b]It plays between Forza Horizon and Carx.[/b]

[b]Pros:[/b]
- Best custom livery edit i've seen lately (window stickers included c'mon forza bite my a$$)
- Nice psychs
- Constantly Updated

[b]Cons:[/b]
- No bodykit customization (yet?)
- Host disconnects and takes everyone with him

[i]If reviews make you question if it's worth it: Totally worth with a discount, but at full price, maybe wait for the devs to improve it further.[/i]

Feels like a drift version of Forza Horizon! Drifting here is a little difficult (i'm using logitech G29), gets better with car upgrades but it's still pretty challenging!
(anyone else using a g29 and found perfect settings do share!)

Map needs major improvement, a route to destination is a must. I have to open the map every few corners to check where I need to go for the next food delivery or camera. Need an option to turn off rotating map as well so it stays in a fixed position.

Another issue is when the host quits the lobby, everyone gets kicked out without warning. Probably will be good to pass on the host to another random player in the lobby instead.

Graphics is awesome as well but it makes a ton of frame drops, some optimization will be needed.

Looking forward to next updates!
It's fun, they had worse physics, but now it's actually playable! I suck at using my wheel, but even with keyboard it's easy
